mnemonics: G-V-E-R-T-E-X: Graphs have Vertices and Edges, Representing TErritories and X-roads
keyPoints: A graph is a non-linear data structure consisting of nodes (vertices) and edges (connections between vertices)
Graphs can be directed (edges have a direction) or undirected (edges have no direction)
Common representations include adjacency matrix (table) and adjacency list (linked lists)
commonMistakes: Confusing directed and undirected graphs when implementing algorithms
Using inefficient representations (e.g., adjacency matrix for sparse graphs)
Forgetting to mark visited nodes in traversal algorithms, causing infinite loops
